summary: Iteration 9 — Frontend-only validation of the new Microsoft Products mega menu and the 'blurred variants' product-page feature on the PHP UCODE SOFTTECH site at https://software-staging.preview.emergentagent.com. Tests covered: (1) homepage hero/stats/Ask-AI banner render with no PHP warnings/notices/errors; (2) Microsoft Products dropdown opens as full-width mega menu with exactly 4 columns (OFFICE FOR PC, OFFICE FOR MAC, WINDOWS, APPS), with all 6 required data-testids present (menu-office-2024-pc, menu-office-2021-pc, menu-office-2019-pc, menu-office-2024-mac, menu-windows-11, menu-microsoft-project) and 37 mega-product-{slug} links rendered; (3) clicking menu-office-2024-pc lands on category.php?slug=office-2024-pc with exactly the 4 expected 2024 PC products and no 2021/2019 leakage; (4) clicking mega-product-windows-11-home opens product.php?slug=windows-11-home; (5) Antivirus dropdown shows 'BITDEFENDER' heading with 6 Bitdefender products and 'MCAFEE' with 1 McAfee+ Premium product; (6) Visio 2024 product page renders Version chips 2024/2021/2019 (all clickable) and OS chips PC + Mac with Mac correctly carrying class 'variant-blur' (blurred, pointer-events:none, data-testid='os-option-mac'); (7) clicking version-option-2021 from Visio 2024 page navigates to Visio 2021 sibling product (verified via href + direct nav); (8) regression — pd-add-to-cart on Windows 11 Home increments cart badge from 0 → 1, /cart.php shows the item, footer shows 'UCODE SOFTTECH LLC' + '101 NW 83rd St, Kansas City, MO 64118'; (9) currency switch USD → EUR changes featured price from $459.99 → € 193.19 (Office 2024 Pro Plus) and dark-mode toggle flips html[data-bs-theme] from 'light' → 'dark'. ONE SPEC DEVIATION found: on product.php?slug=microsoft-office-home-2024-mac, the Edition row contains 4 options (Home / Home and Business / Professional Plus / Home and Student) but NONE of them carry the 'variant-blur' class — the spec requires 'Professional Plus' and 'Home and Student' to be BLURRED for 2024 Mac (since those editions are not available for Mac 2024). Currently all 4 edition chips render as plain clickable links (Professional Plus → PC product, Home and Student → a 2021 Windows product), which contradicts the spec and the documented intent of render_variant_row() in includes/functions.php.

ui_bugs.issue: Edition chips Professional Plus and Home and Student are NOT blurred — they render with class 'btn btn-sm btn-outline-secondary' instead of the required '... variant-blur'. Spec says these two editions are unavailable for Office Home 2024 (Mac) and must be blurred (pointer-events:none). Additionally, Home and Student currently links to a 2021 Windows product (microsoft-office-2021-home-student-windows), which silently changes both the year and OS — confusing for users.

ui_bugs.component: Variant chip 'active' state (Version / Edition / OS rows on all product pages)
ui_bugs.issue: The currently selected variant chip is not visually distinguished — e.g., on /product.php?slug=microsoft-visio-2024-professional-windows-pc the 2024 chip, the PC chip, and the current Edition all carry the same class as the other clickable chips ('btn btn-sm btn-outline-secondary'). The spec language ('2024 (active)', 'PC (active)', 'Mac active') strongly implies an active styling. Consider adding 'active' (or 'btn-primary' / 'fw-bold') to the current selection in render_variant_row().

action_items: In /app/php-version/includes/functions.php render_variant_row() (~line 420) and/or get_variant_group() (~line 307), ensure that on /product.php?slug=microsoft-office-home-2024-mac the Edition values 'Professional Plus' and 'Home and Student' are added to the unavailable set so they render with class 'variant-blur'. Currently only the OS row blurs Mac on Windows-only families — Edition unavailability for cross-OS year combos is not being computed.

Decide whether the unavailable Edition chip should be rendered as a non-link span (preferred — fully non-clickable) OR kept as an <a> with pointer-events:none via .variant-blur. Either way the class must be present.
Add an 'active' CSS class (or swap to btn-primary) to the currently selected Version / Edition / OS chip so users can see which variant is the current product. Today every option looks identical.
Optional polish: confirm that 'Home and Student' for the Mac 2024 page shouldn't link to a 2021 Windows sibling — that cross-jump is confusing if the chip remains clickable.
critical_code_review_comments: Mega menu (header.php lines 76-92) cleanly delegates to nav_microsoft() / render_mega_group() — well-factored, all 4 columns + 37 product links render correctly, all required data-testids are present.
render_mega_group() correctly skips when product_slug_by_name() returns null, preventing dead links — verified 0 broken links in mega menu.
Visio 2024 product page correctly blurs the Mac OS chip (variant-blur applied) — the OS-row blur logic in render_variant_row() works.

The blur logic gap is specifically for the Edition row when the *current* product's (OS, year) combination has fewer editions than other (OS, year) combos. The current implementation appears to collect *all* editions from sibling products regardless of OS/year, then link them — without computing 'is this edition available for THIS os+year'. That computation needs to be added.
Currency switch is server-side via ?cur=EUR and persists on subsequent navigation — verified.
Cart-count badge correctly toggles d-none via cart_count() === 0 check — verified.

No PHP Warning/Notice/Fatal/Parse error strings found on any of the 6 pages visited (index, category x2, product x3, cart). Display_errors appears suppressed or no errors are being emitted, which is the desired prod posture.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% — 10/10 mechanical tests pass, but 1 spec-deviation (edition blur on Office Home 2024 Mac) and 1 UX gap (no 'active' chip styling) remain

test_credentials: Not needed for this iteration — all flows tested as guest. /app/memory/test_credentials.md was not opened; previous iterations recorded admin@ucodesofttechus.com / Admin@UC2026! for admin and TEST_checkout@example.com for buyer.
seed_data_creation: Added 1 line item (Windows 11 Home) to a fresh guest cart session during regression test — no checkout/payment performed. No DB rows created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Mega menu and antivirus dropdown are fully working — focus retest on the Edition-row blur fix for /product.php?slug=microsoft-office-home-2024-mac. The blur logic in functions.php currently only handles the OS axis (Mac blurred when base is Office-Windows-only family at the given year). It does NOT compute Edition × (OS, year) availability. After main agent extends the logic, re-check edition-option-{slug} class lists on both /product.php?slug=microsoft-office-home-2024-mac (expect Professional Plus + Home and Student to be variant-blur) and /product.php?slug=microsoft-office-home-2024-pc (likely no blurs needed there). Also good to add an 'active' marker to the current chip for clearer UX. Tests run from: anonymous Playwright sessions against https://software-staging.preview.emergentagent.com, viewport 1920x1080.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: Open https://software-staging.preview.emergentagent.com/product.php?slug=microsoft-office-home-2024-mac . Inspect the Edition row chips. Expected: chips 'Professional Plus' and 'Home and Student' carry class 'variant-blur' (with pointer-events:none) because those editions are not offered for Office 2024 Mac (Office 2024 Mac SKUs are only Home and Home & Business). Actual: all 4 edition chips render with class 'btn btn-sm btn-outline-secondary' (no variant-blur), and the two unavailable editions link to cross-axis products (Professional Plus → Windows PC; Home and Student → 2021 Windows). RCA: render_variant_row() / get_variant_group() in /app/php-version/includes/functions.php compute availability per (axis value) globally rather than within the current (OS, year) context. For the Version axis the resolver correctly stays within the current OS (so unavailable years blur — verified on Visio 2024), but for the Edition axis the resolver pulls from the full sibling set without filtering by current OS+year, hence no blur. Mitigation: in get_variant_group() when axis='edition', filter the candidate edition values to only those present in sibling products that match the *current* product's OS AND year; any edition that exists in the family but not at this (OS, year) cell should be returned with variant-blur=true and no href (or href to a deterministic sibling but with .variant-blur class). After fix, re-verify with the assertion list in this iteration.
